content: Anya flagged that 1Password annual renewal hits $4,800 on Feb 28. Pushed back to secure SCIM provisioning in contract—non-negotiable for SOC2 Type II audit trail. She'll handle legal review by EOD Feb 14.
summary: 1Password renewal $4.8k; SCIM requirement added to contract terms.

content: SCIM provisioning syncs user identity/role changes from Okta to 1Password automatically. Eliminates manual deprovisioning, cuts offboarding risk by ~90%, and generates audit logs that SOC2 examiners require as evidence.
summary: SCIM auto-syncs user lifecycle; core SOC2 evidence requirement.

content: To enable 1Password SCIM: (1) Confirm contract amendment signed; (2) request API token from 1Password support; (3) configure in Okta → Applications → 1Password; (4) test deprovisioning on Tom (non-critical account); (5) audit log export to DataDog. Document token in 1Password vault, not Slack.
summary: 1Password SCIM enablement checklist for offboarding automation.

content: Priya asked if we're ready to offboard Chris (Sales, left 2/15). Realized we still have his 1Password account active + Cloudflare + Linear access. No SCIM yet. Manually revoked all three by 11am. Noted the gap for audit file.
summary: Manual offboarding for Chris exposed SCIM gap; audit trail logged.

content: SOC2 Type II auditors will ask for 30–90 day access logs proving no orphaned credentials post-exit. SCIM + DataDog audit sink gives us automated, timestamped evidence. Manual revocation logs are weak; they'll request system-level proof.
summary: Auditors require automated, timestamped access revocation logs.

content: Anya confirmed 1Password legal accepted SCIM rider. Invoice issued $4,800. Sebastien signed off on expense. Anya will coordinate with 1Password to activate SCIM on Feb 20; I own Okta integration + testing.
summary: 1Password contract amended; activation scheduled for Feb 20.

content: I've noticed I default to async—write once, reference forever—because re-explaining offboarding / audit requirements burns cognitive cycles. The SCIM setup fits that model: once it's live, the system explains itself via logs. No Slack escalations on 'did we revoke access?'
summary: Async documentation reduces future re-explanation burden.

content: Post-SCIM-activation audit: (1) pull 1Password API activity log for Feb 20–22; (2) cross-check against Okta changelog; (3) verify DataDog ingestion of both logs; (4) generate CSV for SOC2 file; (5) send to Anya for external auditor review by Mar 1.
summary: Post-activation audit protocol for SOC2 evidence compilation.

content: 1Password SCIM live as of 10:15am Feb 20. Tested with Tom's test account: removed from 'Engineering' group in Okta, 1Password vault access revoked within 4 mins. Logs synced to DataDog. No errors. Marked go-live complete in Linear.
summary: SCIM provisioning live; test successful, DataDog integration active.

content: Lesson: vendor contracts should always specify audit/logging requirements upfront. 1Password's SCIM was a bolt-on because we didn't ask during initial procurement. Cost us ~2 weeks of negotiation. Now standard checklist item for next renewal cycles.
summary: Audit requirements belong in initial vendor procurement, not post-hoc.

content: Sebastien asked if SCIM covers 'everything.' Explained it covers identity/role provisioning only—doesn't auto-revoke Cloudflare/Linear/AWS. Those still require manual or separate SCIM connectors. Sent him async summary doc. He acknowledged.
summary: Clarified SCIM scope limits; still need multi-tool offboarding process.
